evals.prompt: Explain which features are most important in my trained XGBoost classifier and give me a beeswarm and bar summary plot.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-shap: builds a SHAP explainer for the tree model (TreeExplainer), computes SHAP values, and produces the beeswarm and bar summary plots to rank global feature importance.

assertions.value: Computes SHAP values for the tree model and generates beeswarm/bar feature-importance plots.

evals.prompt: Why did my model predict this specific customer would churn? Show me a waterfall or force plot for that one prediction.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-shap: computes SHAP values for the single instance and renders a waterfall (or force) plot decomposing the prediction into per-feature contributions from the base value.

assertions.value: Produces a local explanation (waterfall/force plot) for a single prediction's feature contributions.

evals.prompt: Use explainability to check my credit model for bias — I want to see whether sensitive-feature proxies are driving predictions.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-shap: uses SHAP value distributions (e.g. beeswarm/scatter, grouped comparisons) to analyze how features — including proxies for sensitive attributes — drive predictions, supporting a fairness/bias audit.

assertions.value: Applies SHAP to a fairness/bias analysis, examining feature contributions including sensitive-attribute proxies.

evals.prompt: Before I model anything, profile this raw dataset — show distributions, missingness, and pairwise correlations between the columns.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke alterlab-shap; defers to an EDA / data-profiling skill. The ask is exploratory data analysis of a raw dataset (distributions, missingness, correlations), not explaining a trained model's predictions. SHAP requires a fitted model and explains its outputs; it is not a dataset-profiling tool.

assertions.value: Recognizes raw-dataset profiling as EDA territory (no model to explain) and defers rather than invoking SHAP.
